title: Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Manjira wildlife sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary and reservoir located in the Sangareddy district of Telangana State, India.
overview.description: Manjira wildlife sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary and reservoir located in the Sangareddy district of Telangana State, India. Originally a crocodile sanctuary developed to protect the vulnerable species mugger crocodile, today it is home to more than 70 species of birds. The reservoir, located in the sanctuary, provides drinking water to Hyderabad and Secundarabad.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Manjira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kodur, Pulkal, Pulkal, Ramalingeswara Temple, Nandikandi, Sadasivpet.
